title: [lichen_l2.c:814-828] NET_L2_MULTICAST flags assume true broadcast medium
description:
Gap found: L2 flags assumptions not verified against all LoRa configurations.

Why this was missed: Protocol assumptions about LoRa broadcast not examined.

The actual bug: Lines 814-828 set NET_L2_MULTICAST and NET_L2_MULTICAST_SKIP_JOIN_SOLICIT_NODE flags based on the assumption that 'LoRa is inherently broadcast - all transmissions reach all receivers'.

This assumption holds for:
- Standard LoRa (no addressing)
- LoRaWAN Class A/B/C devices (receive windows)

But may not hold for:
- LoRa devices with hardware address filtering
- LoRa mesh with forwarding (may not receive all traffic)
- LoRaWAN Class B/C with specific downlinks

The LICHEN design (over standard LoRa) does use true broadcast, so this is correct. But the comment should note this assumption for future maintainers who might use LoRaWAN.

Priority 4 - documentation note.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation note about broadcast medium assumption for NET_L2_MULTICAST flags in lichen_l2.c. Explains that the assumption holds for standard LoRa but may not apply to LoRaWAN or hardware address filtering configurations.
